channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Agbonayinma Resigns From APC Amid Allegations of Undue Interference
item.description: Ehiozuwa Johnson Agbonayinma, a former member of the House of Representatives who represented Egor/Ikpoba-Okha Federal Constituency, has announced his resignation from the All Progressives Congress (A...

item.title: Maharashtra's Leopard Contraception Trial Aims to Reduce Human-Leopard Conflict
item.description: In Maharashtra, a state pioneering the use of immunocontraception in leopards, the government aims to reduce the rising conflict between humans and leopards. The Ministry of Environment, Forests, and ...

item.title: Bangladesh Requests Increased Fuel Supplies from India Amid Improved Relations
item.description: Bangladesh has requested an increase in fuel imports from India, marking a significant diplomatic outreach as the two nations work towards improving ties. During a recent visit to New Delhi, Banglades...

item.title: Correctional staff seize meth and cannabis worth over $100,000 at Manitoba federal prison
item.description: Correctional Service of Canada (CSC) staff at Stony Mountain Institution, located north of Winnipeg, intercepted methamphetamine, cannabis, and a cannabis-containing concentrate from entering the fede...

item.title: Pakistan's Key Role in Securing Ceasefire Between US and Iran
item.description: Pakistan has positioned itself as a crucial intermediary in brokering peace between the US and Iran, achieving a temporary ceasefire in the ongoing Middle East conflict. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif ...

item.title: Israel Airstrikes Hit Central Beirut Despite Iran-US Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: Israel launched multiple airstrikes in central Beirut on Wednesday, killing dozens and injuring hundreds, just hours after a two-week ceasefire was agreed between Iran, the US, and Israel. The ceasefi...

item.title: Rwanda Calls for International Laws to Prevent Genocide Ideology and Hate Speech
item.description: During the 32nd commemoration of the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi in Nairobi, Rwanda's High Commissioner to Kenya, Ernest Rwamucyo, called for urgent global action to combat genocide ideology, deni...

item.title: Australia’s manufacturing sector remains stable despite political rhetoric
item.description: Despite claims that Australia has deindustrialised, the notion that manufacturing is dead is misleading. The article argues that Australia’s manufacturing sector has evolved rather than disappeared, w...

item.title: OpenSSL 3.6.2 Security Update Addresses Eight Vulnerabilities and Fixes Regressions
item.description: OpenSSL has released version 3.6.2, addressing eight security vulnerabilities across multiple components. The most severe issue is rated as Moderate. Key fixes include incorrect failure handling in RS...

item.title: Australia’s recession risk amid Trump, Iran and RBA policy
item.description: A lot of economists and markets are talking up a possible recession in Australia, but the article’s author argues the country isn’t there yet. The main worries come from Donald Trump’s threats of war ...

item.title: UK Pastor Faces Manslaughter Charges Following Baptism Accident
item.description: A British pastor is facing manslaughter charges after a man drowned during a baptism ceremony, according to authorities. The incident occurred at a church service where the baptism was being conducted...

item.title: Practical ways to cut grocery costs without compromising nutrition
item.description: With rising interest rates, higher fuel costs and global conflicts pushing up grocery prices, many Australian households are finding it tougher to keep food spending under control. This article offers...

item.title: Why USD 100 Oil Prices May Not Be as Devastating as Before
item.description: The article explores the impact of rising oil prices on the global economy, particularly in light of the ongoing conflicts in the Middle East. While many have expected a repeat of the 1970s oil crises...

item.title: US and Iran reach temporary ceasefire, Strait of Hormuz set to reopen
item.description: The United States and Iran have reached a two-week ceasefire agreement just hours before a major deadline set by President Donald Trump, who warned Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face severe...

item.title: OpenAI introduces a blueprint to strengthen child protection against AI-related abuse
item.description: In response to growing concerns about the safety of children online, OpenAI has released a comprehensive Child Safety Blueprint aimed at reducing AI-enabled child exploitation in the United States. Th...

item.title: Rivers State Education Commissioner Thanks STAN Executives for Support Following Appointment
item.description: The Rivers State Commissioner for Education, Dr. Peters Nwagor, don show big appreciation for the executive members of the Science Teachers Association of Nigeria (STAN), Rivers State Chapter. Dem vis...

item.title: Legal Standoff Over Gachagua's Kikuyu Rally Amid Police Resistance
item.description: Plans for a weekend rally by Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP) leader Rigathi Gachagua in Kikuyu have sparked a legal and political tussle with police. On Tuesday, Gachagua's allies faced resista...

item.title: Top Universities for International Relations: A Ranking Analysis
item.description: So, you're thinking about a career in international relations, eh? A good start is choosing the right university, right? This article, which seems to be focused on global university rankings, presumab...

item.title: Canada announces one-time GST top-up payment for eligible Canadians this spring
item.description: The Canadian government has introduced the Canada Groceries and Essentials Benefit, formerly known as the GST credit, to provide additional financial support to low- and modest-income Canadians. This ...

item.title: F1 Engineers Meet to Review New Engine Regulations After Initial Races
item.description: The top technical minds in Formula One are gathering this week to discuss the impact of the new engine rules after three races into the 2026 season. The main topics on the table will include energy ma...
